title: Eva in a sentence
summary:
[]: Eva in a sentence             Quote Post by Xacebans » Thu Dec 01, 2011 4:45 pm How would you describe Evangelion in just one sentence? Here's mine: "Freudian symbolism ensues as a boy pilots his mother." And another: "Evangelion is a popular Japanese fanfic of the Bible."

[]:             Quote Post by Aiko Heiwa » Thu Dec 01, 2011 4:56 pm Evangelion is oh

chunks.content:
Article: Eva in a sentence
[]: Eva in a sentence             Quote Post by Xacebans » Thu Dec 01, 2011 4:45 pm How would you describe Evangelion in just one sentence? Here's mine: "Freudian symbolism ensues as a boy pilots his mother." And another: "Evangelion is a popular Japanese fanfic of the Bible."

[]:             Quote Post by Aiko Heiwa » Thu Dec 01, 2011 4:56 pm Evangelion is oh god what the fuck did i just watch.

[]:             Quote Post by Alastor » Thu Dec 01, 2011 4:59 pm "If Devilman and Ideon had a baby, raised by Ultraman (and possibly Skull Killer Jakiou), that baby would grow up to be Evangelion"

[]:             Quote Post by Tankred » Thu Dec 01, 2011 5:19 pm My son is piloting his mother... just as planned.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Thu Dec 01, 2011 5:25 pm Evangelion is something, that after you have finished watching will make you sexually frustrated.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Dec 01, 2011 5:26 pm (read my sig)

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Thu Dec 01, 2011 5:37 pm NemZ wrote: (read my sig)

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Thu Dec 01, 2011 5:47 pm Real Answer: "Animation can explain whatever the mind of man can conceive. This facility makes it the most versatile and explicit means of communication yet devised for quick mass appreciation."-Walt Disney Joke Answer: "Not bad! But is it art?"-Bugs Bunny

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Thu Dec 01, 2011 7:30 pm An anime that defines the word "Social Phenomenon", to such a level that when Eva is mentioned in Japan it is widely understood as the anime not some personality from the Bible, and this will likely not happen again; it is also a historic anime that made "midnight anime" and the current generation of otaku anime possible with its influence still broadly felt in anime and manga these days.

Article: Eva in a sentence
 not some personality from the Bible, and this will likely not happen again; it is also a historic anime that made "midnight anime" and the current generation of otaku anime possible with its influence still broadly felt in anime and manga these days.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireand'chutes77 » Thu Dec 01, 2011 7:59 pm " Evangelion is a giant robot saga the same way Twin Peaks was a cop show." or: " Neon Genesis Evangelion is basically one extended therapy session masquerading as a Humongous Mecha series, with the masquerade slowly collapsing towards the end." ~ TV Tropes

[]:             Quote Post by AngelNo13Bardiel » Thu Dec 01, 2011 8:27 pm " Um...well...it's kind of a mecha show, but at the same time, it's not, so...ah what the hell, good friggin' luck to your sanity by the time you get to EoE, sir/ma'am. " Fireand'chutes77 wrote: " Evangelion is a giant robot saga the same way Twin Peaks was a cop show."

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Dec 01, 2011 9:01 pm Evangelion is an exercise in metatxuality, in that it creates a sensation in the viewer through interaction with the text as much as the text itself.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Thu Dec 01, 2011 10:08 pm Evangelion is all about Shinji Ikari.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Dec 02, 2011 1:48 am Here's one I prepared earlier... It's a story about multiple notionally allied factions competing to launch a Singularity bootstrapped on alien technology found under Hakone in Japan and in the Antarctic from the point of view of a boy caught up in all the machinations.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Fri Dec 02, 2011 4:38 am It prints money.

Article: Eva in a sentence

[]:             Quote Post by Fireand'chutes77 » Fri Dec 02, 2011 5:21 am CJD wrote: "Eva is one of those shows you have to watch once to get the premise, twice to get the jist, thrice to understand, and then go to EvaGeeks to realize you didn't understand it at all."

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Fri Dec 02, 2011 6:30 am Fireand'chutes77 wrote: Fixed. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Fri Dec 02, 2011 6:42 am Evangelion, the world's best Pyramid Scheme.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Dec 02, 2011 1:37 pm Haha! I went to school, for this! "A timid 14-year-old boy is forced against his will to pilot the Evangelion in order to defeat the Angels."
